steps.title: Simplify the second fraction
steps.content:
Factor the denominator of the second fraction:
$$
x^2y-12xy=xy(x-12)
$$
Then cancel the common factor, using the restrictions from the original denominators:
$$
\frac{y(x-12)}{xy(x-12)}=\frac{1}{x}
$$

steps.title: Combine the fractions
steps.content:
The expression becomes:
$$
\frac{y+17}{x-12}+\frac{1}{x}
$$
Use the common denominator $x(x-12)$ and combine the numerators:
$$
\frac{x(y+17)}{x(x-12)}+\frac{x-12}{x(x-12)}=\frac{xy+18x-12}{x(x-12)}
$$

steps.title: Match the choice format
steps.content: The choices use a denominator with the factor $y$ included, so multiply numerator and denominator by $y$: $$ \frac{y(xy+18x-12)}{xy(x-12)}=\frac{xy^2+18xy-12y}{x^2y-12xy} $$ The answer is C.
